LD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD

The LD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD structure is used in the XLaunchNewImage function to specify the reason that the Xbox Dashboard is being launched by the title, and what action the Dashboard should take upon launch.

typedef struct _LD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD {
    DWORD dwReason;
    DWORD dwContext;
    DWORD dwParameter1;
    DWORD dwParameter2;
    BYTE  Reserved[MAX_LAUNCH_DATA_SIZE - 16];
} LD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD, *PLD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD;

Members

dwReason

The reason the Dashboard is being launched. dwReason must be one of the following values:

ValueMeaning
XLD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD_MAIN_MENULaunch to the Dashboard's main menu.
XLD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD_ERRORLaunch to the Dashboard for the Dashboard to indicate an error to the user and instruct the user on how to proceed.
XLD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD_MEMORYLaunch to the Dashboard memory and storage area so that the user can delete save games or other titles in order to provide necessary space for the calling title.
XLD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD_SETTINGSLaunch to the Dashboard's settings menu so that the user can adjust the console settings.
XLD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD_MUSICLaunch to the Dashboard's music playback area.

For Xbox Live titles, one of the following values (or one of the values listed above) may be specified:

ValueMeaning
XLD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD_NETWORK_CONFIGURATIONLaunch to the Dashboard's network configuration menu.
XLD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD_NEW_ACCOUNT_SIGNUPLaunch to the Dashboard's new account section.
XLD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD_ACCOUNT_MANAGEMENTLaunch to the Dashboard's account management section to display user messages that the user may be required to read before proceeding with online play.
XLD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD_ONLINE_MENULaunch to the Dashboard's main online menu.

Note   When launching the Dashboard with XLD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD_ACCOUNT_MANAGEMENT, Xbox Live titles should not close the online logon task handle (by calling XOnlineTaskClose) before calling XLaunchNewImage. The title should have a valid, active Xbox Live session when rebooting into the Dashboard, so that the Dashboard can use the logon state to determine user messages or other problems. In any case, titles are not required to close task handles before rebooting to the Dashboard or content downloader, or before calling XOnlineTitleUpdate to boot to the updater. However, as for any call to XLaunchNewImage, the title should be performing minimal processing, with no background or asynchronous tasks pending, and no activity in other threads. See the Quick-Booting section of the XTL Overview for more information.

dwContext
A title-defined value that will be returned to the title in the LD_FROM_DASHBOARD structure (see XGetLaunchInfo) when the Dashboard reboots to the title after performing whatever service the title has asked of the Dashboard.
dwParameter1
Parameter passed to the Dashboard. The meaning of this parameter depends on the value specified in dwReason (see Remarks below).
dwParameter2
Parameter passed to the Dashboard. The meaning of this parameter depends on the value specified in dwReason (see Remarks below).
Reserved
Reserved; do not use.

Remarks

When a title launches the Xbox Dashboard using XLaunchNewImage, the LD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD structure is used to tell the Dashboard why the title is launching it and what action the Dashboard should perform.

Unless otherwise specified below, dwParameter1 and dwParameter2 should both be zero.

If dwReason is XLD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD_MEMORY, dwParameter1 is the drive letter where memory blocks need to be freed (for example, 'U'), and dwParameter2 is the total number of blocks needed (not the number of additional blocks needed beyond what is already available).

If dwReason is XLD_LAUNCH_DASHBOARD_SETTINGS, dwParameter1 may have zero or more of the following flags set, indicating which settings should be adjusted by the user:
ValueMeaning
XLD_SETTINGS_CLOCKAdjust the clock.
XLD_SETTINGS_TIMEZONEChange the timezone.
XLD_SETTINGS_LANGUAGEChange the current language setting.
XLD_SETTINGS_VIDEOChange the current video settings.
XLD_SETTINGS_AUDIOChange the current audio settings.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xbox.h, for include use Xtl.h.

See Also

XGetLaunchInfo, XLaunchNewImage, LD_FROM_DASHBOARD
